summaries.text: Student of Socrates whose works will be foundational to understanding wisdom and meaning.
Student of Socrates who records his teachings and philosophy.
Ancient Greek philosopher who developed comprehensive theory of psyche and enlightenment.
Aristotle's teacher whose ideas he builds upon and revises.
Philosophical ancestor whose anagogy and theory of forms are integrated into Neoplatonic metaphysics.
Exemplar of sacred inexhaustibility for Vervaeke; endless return and deepening without final form.
Inner conflict and multiple motivational centers; foundational to understanding anxiety and modal confusion.
Ancient Greek philosopher whose work on meaning and flourishing will be discussed.
Ancient Greek philosopher who developed theory of growth, character, and human development.
Ancient philosopher whose conformity theory and worldview are detailed.
Whose conformity theory of knowing and levels of actuality/potentiality become central to Neoplatonic epistemology.
Ancient Greek philosopher. Distinction between sophia and phronesis foundational to contemporary wisdom research. Vervaeke argues for their opponent relationship.
Foundational caring and dasein concepts resonant with relevance realization understanding.
On primordiality of being-in-the-world and foundational caring; influences understanding of religio.
Central prophetic figure. Philosopher of phenomenology and being. Diagnoses metaphysical tradition as history of nihilism. Proposes phenomenological exploration of dasein as response.
Central figure. Deepens phenomenology beyond Husserl through participatory knowing, dasein, and ontology. Diagnoses metaphysics as nihilism.
Philosophical predecessor to Tillich and Barfield on being and language.
Collaborator on cognitive modeling of parasitic processing in Buddhist psychology.
Psychometrist collaborator on connecting relevance realization to general intelligence.
Co-author with Vervaeke; wisdom involves seeing through illusion into more-real.
Collaborative theorist with Vervaeke on wisdom. Together developing convergence argument showing relevance realization at core of wisdom.
Co-developer with Vervaeke of 2013 wisdom model. Collaborative work on flow, mindfulness, and wisdom integration through relevance realization.
Modern psychologist whose work on consciousness and meaning will be referenced.
Psychologist who identified stuckness and existential inertia as primary reasons people enter therapy.
Psychologist responding to meaning crisis through integration of shadow, individuation, and archetypal symbolism. Influenced by gnostics and Kant.
Analytical psychologist discovering sacred depths of psyche through archetypes and individuation. Gnostic and alchemical influences inform psychological framework.
Proposes cognitive science as the framework for understanding meaning-making machinery and developing responses to the meaning crisis grounded in science rather than ideology.
Demonstrates how problem formulation and insight are central to understanding intelligence and rationality, not as algorithms but as skillful navigation of combinatorial complexity.
Orchestrates a convergence analysis showing relevance realization is central to intelligence, consciousness, and meaning-making.
Constructs a naturalistic theory of relevance realization grounded in enactive cognition, salience tagging, and autopoetic systems.
Neoplatonic Christian mystic; 'the eye by which I see God is the eye by which God sees me.'
Neoplatonic Christian mystic; eye of seeing and being seen as reciprocal cosmic perspective.
Rhineland mystic influencing German gnosis tradition. Gnostic elements entering through Heidegger connecting mysticism to philosophical phenomenology.
Rhineland mystic whose union mysticism parallels Sufi themes in Corbin's work.
Philosopher quoted on the persistence of grammatical God-language.
Takes Schopenhauer's will to power and attempts to redeem it as creative self-transcendence, but remains trapped within the Romantic irrationalism he inherits.
On looking into abyss; sacredness of transgressive experience.
On staring into abyss; transgressive aspect of sacredness.
Modern theologian whose The Courage To Be articulates the distinction between fear and anxiety central to understanding these philosophies.
Theologian of the meaning crisis. The Courage To Be announces crisis and revises theology through existential philosophy. God beyond theism; connection to gnosis.
Theologian whose God-beyond-theism parallels Jung's sacred psyche concept.
Theologian of meaning crisis and ultimate concern. Courage To Be announces crisis and proposes theonomous response. Gnostic elements in God-beyond-God.
Wisdom in psychology; pioneering cognitive scientific study of wisdom.
Pioneered cognitive science of wisdom; convergence between philosophical and psychological theories.
Psychologist with work on wisdom theories and cognitive styles involved in wise judgment. Referenced in Berlin paradigm discussion.

Pivotal wisdom researcher connecting psychology to pedagogy. Balance theory shows wisdom as tacit understanding optimizing fit to context. Connects wisdom to meaning in life through virtue and mastery.
Ancient Greek philosopher central to the Western tradition of wisdom and self-knowledge.
Greek philosopher who epitomized the Axial Revolution through the pursuit of wisdom and self-knowledge.
Ancient Greek philosopher whose life and death exemplify the Axial quest for wisdom and self-knowledge.
Plato's teacher whose death inspired Plato's philosophical project.
The Buddha, whose insights into suffering and meaning are central to the series.
The Buddha whose mythological journey illustrates modal confusion and transformation.
The Buddha-to-be whose mythological journey illustrates the Axial Revolution in India.
The Buddha whose enlightenment exemplifies non-dual consciousness.
Aspiration; overcoming existential ignorance through developmental process of becoming.
Philosopher discussing aspirational rationality and how optimization of cognition tends to shift and change goals we pursue. Expands Stanovich's account.
Philosopher developing aspiration theory. Incremental transformative process acquiring values one doesn't yet possess. Proleptic rationality makes aspiration itself rational.
Philosopher who analyzed the great dis-embedding of the Axial Revolution.
Philosopher who analyzes conformity theory and worldview.
Philosopher of contact epistemology whose work underlies the understanding of optimal grip as conformity to reality.
Contemporary philosopher who analyzed bullshit as distinct from lying.
Philosopher who articulated the concept of the unthinkable—viable alternatives we cannot make livable to ourselves.
Concept of wanton behavior; motivation through conflicting desires undermining agency.
Co-founder of cognitive problem-solving research, whose formalization of problem spaces reveals the machinery of how we solve problems.
Co-founder of cognitive science of problem-solving; conducted research showing that problem formulation (not just solution application) is crucial to solving complex problems.
Economist and cognitive scientist introducing bounded rationality concept, showing humans cannot be purely computational or algorithmic due to realistic constraints.
Philosopher of embodied understanding and expertise.
Phenomenologist influencing wisdom research on problem finding and relevance realization as core cognitive mechanisms.
Contemporary interpreter of Heidegger helping translate his ideas into cognitive science framework.
Religious figure whose teachings relate to meaning and transformation.
Introduces agape as God's creative love and offers metanoia—radical personal awakening—as path to transformation.
Incarnates kairos and embodies agape as the way to personal transformation and constitution of personhood.
Philosopher who identified the problem of circular explanation in biology.
His framework that the mind filters reality through categories sets the stage for Romantic inversion—seeking reality by moving into the irrational rather than the rational.
Hegel responds to Kant's critical philosophy by rejecting the thing-in-itself and making reality identical to intelligibility.

Cognitive scientist arguing rationality involves valuing cognitive processes in addition to products. Central to the rationality debate, distinguishing rationality from both intelligence and logic. Work frames rationality as overcoming self-deception.

Central figure responding brilliantly to Cohen, Cherniak, and Smedslund. Distinguishes intelligence from rationality, proposes dual processing model, and identifies psychotechnology and cognitive style as missing pieces of rationality.

Proposes dual processing model where S2 has corrective function for S1. Identifies psychotechnology and active open-mindedness as missing pieces for rationality. Vervaeke critiques as incomplete regarding insight.
Philosopher of transformative experience who shows how major life changes create epistemic impossibility of rational decision.
Transformative experience; cannot reason through epistemic gaps; need anagoge.
Philosopher of transformative experience. Sudden, non-inferential transformation. Inspires aspiration but doesn't explain incremental path.
Philosopher who developed theory of focal and subsidiary awareness in attention.
On subsidiary and focal awareness; on indwelling; on holding things in mind through participatory engagement.
Indwelling; proceeding through the tool toward participatory knowing.
On linguistic evolution and changing meaning; metaphor as fundamental to language.
Philosopher of participation influenced by romantics. Participatory imagination central to recovering meaning and sacred dimension.
Philosopher of language and imagination. Recovers participatory consciousness and poiesis. Influenced by Coleridge and romanticism.
Personalizes Jesus's teachings, projecting inner psychological conflict onto cosmic narrative of divine struggle and redemption.
Undergoes transformative experience on Damascus road, personalizes agape-metanoia, but projects inner conflict onto cosmic structure.
Apostle whose letters Augustine encounters, showing how agape heals inner conflict and constitutes transformation.
Meaning in life as connectedness to something larger; symbolic expressions of deeper transjective yearning.
Meaning in life as connectedness; identifies crisis of objective attractiveness in scientific worldview.
Philosopher on meaning in life. Her argument that meaning is irreducible to morality influences Ardelt and Sternberg's accounts.
Co-founder with Simon of the cognitive science of problem-solving, whose work on general problem solvers provides the foundation for understanding intelligence.
Co-founder of cognitive science; proposed the general problem solver model and formalization of problem spaces.
His book Descartes' Error critiques Descartes' separation of reason from emotion, though Vervaeke argues Descartes actually anticipated these criticisms.
Descartes' Error; emotions essential to cognition; without emotion, incapacitation despite intact calculation.
Inverts Kant to argue that will, not reason, is fundamental, making existence inherently tragic and nihilistic.
His critique that Hegel ignores the will and the irrational aspects of human existence points to what systematic reason cannot capture.
Church father who synthesizes Neoplatonism and Christianity, creating framework for medieval understanding of meaning, reality, and transformation.
His synthesis of Christian theology with Platonic and Aristotelian thought exemplifies the three orders framework that the scientific revolution will dismantle.
His recognition that the new cosmos is terrifying and that certainty cannot alleviate existential dread foreshadows the inadequacy of Descartes' response.
Recognizes that both Descartes' certainty-seeking and Hobbes' computationalism miss something essential—the transformative, embodied, participatory dimensions of knowing.
Collaborator on the Journal of Logic and Computation article on relevance realization and emergent frameworks in cognitive science.
Co-author on relevance realization paper showing AI convergence with cognitive science theory.
Naturalist whose evolution theory exemplifies dynamical systems.
Theory of evolution via natural selection provides the foundational analogy for understanding relevance realization as non-essentialist, process-driven optimization.
Argued that rationality consists in selecting relevant implications from the combinatorially explosive set of possible ones.

Neuroscientist arguing we face finitary predicament making algorithmic rationality impossible. Rationality must accommodate computational limitations. Stanovich accepts this but relocates it to intelligence rather than rationality.
Philosopher of attention who explains attention as optimization rather than spotlight.
Philosopher of attention who explained attention as optimization of other processes.
Anthropologist who developed concept of existential mode.
On religion's meta-meaning function protecting against domicide; meta-assimilation aspect of sacredness.
On prajña and non-duality; scaling up and down simultaneously; perspective interpenetration.
On prajña and simultaneous scaling up and down; perspective interpenetration in non-dual wisdom.
His concept of 'deepity' helps identify how we use language to appear profound while actually bullshitting ourselves about meaning.
Provided the robot example illustrating how action requires determining relevant side effects.
Cognitive scientist who studied implicit knowing and expertise.
Third-generation cognitive science; emphasizes optimal gripping and being-in-the-world.
Founder of phenomenology. Pursued contact epistemology through intentionality and world disclosure. Heidegger deepens by adding participatory knowing and existential modes.
Heidegger's teacher and critic. Phenomenology remains Cartesian; lacks participatory knowing and ontological grounding.
Cognitive psychologist who studied soft vigilance and mindful attention.
Psychologist who studied soft vigilance as alternative to concentrated attention.
Scholar of Aristotle on purpose and structural functional organization.
Contemporary philosopher whose interpretation illuminates how Neoplatonism integrates being with knowing and realness with understanding.
Scholar of Islamic philosophy and symbolism. Addresses meaning crisis through imaginal realm and symbolic thinking. Deeply influenced by gnosis.
Islamic philosophy scholar and mystical theologian. Recovers imaginal realm against materialist scientism. Integrates Sufi, Gnostic, and Neoplatonic traditions.
On reflectiveness gap and the optimization problem between immersion (wanton action) and reflection (Hamlet's paralysis).
On solving reflectiveness gap through Daoism and flow; dynamic integration rather than static balance.
Developmental psychology founder; assimilation and accommodation parallel compression and particularization.

Developmental psychologist whose conservation task experiments reveal systematic errors reflecting competence deficits, not performance errors. Provides model for understanding transframing as developmental breakthrough.
Philosopher of wisdom distinguishing descriptive knowledge from interpretive knowledge. Interpretive knowledge involves grasping significance, essential to understanding.
Philosopher whose distinction between descriptive and interpretive knowledge is central to Ardelt's theory. Interpretive knowledge relates understanding and significance grasping.
His distinction between weak and strong AI clarifies that computational power alone may not explain consciousness.
Identified that representations are aspectual—they select relevant features rather than capturing all properties.

Originator of active open-mindedness concept. Emphasizes optimization rather than maximization of bias correction, since overdoing it creates its own problems. Need for cognition predicts cultivating this style.
Originator of active open-mindedness. Emphasizes optimization principle: don't maximize bias correction or you'll overwhelm yourself. Clarifies need for cognition as problem-seeking motivation.
Coherence; freeing communication from cultural grammar; distributed cognition and collective intelligence.
Coherence and meta-psychotechnology; freeing communication from constraining cultural grammar.
His rejection of Hegel's idealism and turn to material conditions and class struggle reveals the passivity and abstraction of Hegelian thought.
His dialectical materialism offers competing secular utopia to Nazism, equally totalizing and equally devastating in historical consequence.

Philosopher in rationality debate arguing humans cannot be fundamentally irrational. Proposes competence-performance distinction, suggesting all experimental errors are performance errors, not competence failures.

Philosopher in rationality debate arguing autonomy of reason prevents us from being fundamentally irrational. Standards must originate from us. However, this doesn't prevent competence-level errors when we have multiple competencies.
Argued that rules cannot fully specify their own application and that skill of judgment is fundamental to following rules.
On family resemblance and categories without essences; critiques essentialism for natural language concepts.
Mathematical work showing optimization between efficiency and resiliency in networks.
Mathematical proof that networks optimize trade-offs between efficiency and resiliency.
His doctrine of sola fide and attack on tradition radicalizes the isolation of the self while paradoxically training culture in narcissism through the promise of unearned positive regard.
His anti-Semitism provides theological precedent and language for Nazi ideology, showing how religious fragmentation enables totalitarian manipulation.
Cognitive scientist who developed theory of exaptation in brain function.
On exaptation in brain; neural reuse and circuit reconfiguration for new functions.
Researcher arguing problem finding is central to wisdom and creativity. Good problem finders identify nexuses—core problems whose solution impacts multiple domains.
Researcher on problem finding. Central to wisdom and creativity; good finders identify problem nexuses.
Lifespan developmental psychologist. Co-creator of Berlin paradigm for wisdom. Initiated empirical investigation of wisdom through five criteria and experimental tasks.
Lifespan psychologist with Staudinger developing Berlin paradigm. Vervaeke criticizes focus on product theory without process theory accounting for transformational development.
Philosopher who recovered understanding of ancient philosophy as spiritual exercises rather than abstract doctrines.
Philosopher whose work on ancient philosophy as spiritual practice influences Vervaeke's understanding of medieval transformation.
Third-century philosopher who created integrated spiritual and philosophical system integrating Plato, Aristotle, and Stoicism.
Neoplatonist whose mystical experience Augustine discovers, providing path of agapic transformation through love.
Greek philosopher who discovered mathematical patterns and invented the term 'cosmos'.
Influenced Plato's understanding of self-transcendence and mathematical reality.

His attempt to solve the meaning crisis through mathematical method and computational certainty provides an alternative to Luther's faith, but one equally divorced from embodied, participatory reality.
Defends the immaterial mind against Hobbes using arguments derived from the scientific revolution itself, revealing deep contradictions in how science frames the mind-body relationship.
Ethics as extended meditation for scientia intuitiva; symbols as means to cosmic perspective.
Scientia intuitiva and Ethics as extended meditation; recovering sacredness within Cartesian worldview.
Contemporary Buddhist scholar who understands mindfulness as recovery of being mode.
Buddhist scholar who argues for understanding Buddhism existentially rather than as belief system, influencing Vervaeke's reinterpretation.
His work on qualia and subjective experience extends Descartes' argument that consciousness poses problems for purely material theories of mind.
Analysis of absurdity; arguments for absurdity are after-the-fact expressions rather than generators; perspectival clash is root.
His concept of the metacrisis identifies how ecological, economic, and spiritual crises are interconnected and cannot be solved through politics alone.
Meta-crisis concept; integrated crises requiring coordinated response.
Developmental psychologist with Baltes. Co-developed Berlin wisdom paradigm. Created empirical methodology for measuring wisdom in experimental contexts.
Co-developer of Berlin paradigm empirical approach to wisdom. Emphasizes metaheuristics and pragmatic orchestration of mind and virtue.
His synthesis of Romanticism, Gnosticism, will to power, and nationalist mythology into a totalitarian pseudo-religious ideology that exterminates millions.
Researcher who studied hyperbolic discounting.
Religion for Atheists; secular exploration of religion's psychotechnological functions.
Contemporary philosopher whose dynamical systems theory was inspired by Aristotle.
Plotinus's teacher in the Platonic tradition from whom he developed his comprehensive philosophical system.
Neuroscientist whose research on enlightenment experiences and brain imaging provides empirical backing for continuity between insight and mystical states.
On salience imbalance in metaphorical understanding.
Philosopher and cognitive scientist on human use of tools.
Socratic student who learns to converse with himself, pioneering the internalization of Socratic elenchus that becomes Stoic practice.
Medieval theologian who attempts to preserve medieval synthesis against Aristotle's challenge by introducing nature/supernatural division.
Introduces finitary predicament concept showing formal logic and probability cannot be comprehensively applied in life due to combinatorial explosion.
Cognitive psychologist who studied implicit learning.
The New Inquisitions; history of heresy persecution foreshadowing totalitarian regimes.

Psychologist studying practical wisdom. With Sharpe, argues virtues form interdependent system centered on relevance. Advocates phronesis over legislation. Critical of replacing wisdom cultivation with rules.
Proposer of global workspace theory, which provides a key account of consciousness as a mechanism for bringing information into coordinated processing.
Addresses perennial problems through psychotechnologies; duka and modal confusion as core targets.

Psychologist studying mindset. Shows fixed versus malleable beliefs about intelligence profoundly affect need for cognition, problem-solving, and tendency toward self-deception. Makes rationality existential rather than merely cognitive.
Discovered general intelligence factor (g) and strong positive manifold; foundational to psychometrics.
Co-author with McKee establishing that wisdom centrally involves systematic insight and transframing, drawing on Piagetian developmental psychology to explain competence shifts.
His heliocentric model, defended not on empirical grounds but on mathematical elegance, marks the first major shift toward prioritizing abstract symbolism over sensory experience.
2004 binocular rivalry experiments linking consciousness to neural synchrony-asynchrony transitions.
Collaborated with Simon on the mutilated chessboard experiment demonstrating how reframing turns combinatorially explosive problems into tractable ones.
Psychologist who developed flow theory and studied optimal experience.
Co-author of Relevance Theory; argues that all conversational maxims reduce to the single principle of being relevant.
Cognitive psychologist developing dual process theory with book Thinking, Fast and Slow. Provides empirical basis for S1/S2 distinction.
Famous for formulating the hard problem of consciousness, which Vervaeke acknowledges but does not attempt to solve.
Scholar of Upper Paleolithic transition and shamanism.
Researcher who demonstrated through surveys that people reporting higher state experiences show significant improvement in multiple life dimensions.
Co-author of Relevance Theory with Sperber; advanced the view that relevance is central to both communication and cognition.
Philosophy of biology; niche construction and extended evolutionary synthesis.
Cynic philosopher whose provocative demonstrations (lamp search for honest man, public masturbation) challenge cultural assumptions.
His work on practical induction and the balance between convergence and elegance provides philosophical foundations for understanding plausibility.
Stoic philosopher whose distinction of control and non-control becomes foundational to understanding wisdom in any circumstances.
Epicurean philosopher who diagnoses anxiety about death and proposes acceptance of mortality combined with philosophical friendship.
His proposal of 'synergy' between human and divine effort in salvation is rejected by Luther, crystallizing the Lutheran rejection of participatory collaboration.
20th-century psychoanalyst who distinguished having and being modes.
Deep continuity hypothesis; mind-in-life framework; development of 4E cognitive science.
Third-generation 4E cognitive science founder; deep resonance between neuroscience and Buddhist dharma.
Applies Romantic thinking to theology, attempting to reconstruct religion around feeling and imagination.
On mystery and sensibility transcendence; transframing as trajectory of opening.

The central figure in the lecture, Galileo's use of abstract mathematics to describe physical reality and his discovery of inertial motion fundamentally reframe the universe as mechanistic and meaning-free.
The central figure attempting a total synthesis of science, faith, and philosophy; his system becomes the template for modern totalizing ideologies.
On metaphors we live by; conceptual metaphor theory; critiqued for oversimplifying projection mechanism.
Author of 'How to Solve It'; distinguished between algorithms and heuristics in the context of problem-solving techniques.
Visual perception theorist whose concept of affordances is central to understanding how consciousness reveals what is actionable in the world.
Author of The Ancestral Mind. Argues in therapy you need to trigger insight through S1 processing, not infer your way through transformative change.
Developed the theory of conversational implicature showing that communication depends on conveying more than is said through relevance.
Developed the concept of the skill of judgment as fundamental to rationality and rule-following.
Philosopher of understanding. Standard of effectiveness: understanding enables application and problem finding across domains. Context-relative and contextually sensitive.
Cognitive scientist who articulated the concept of optimal grip and its role in skilled expertise across all domains of human engagement.
2014 comprehensive review establishing self-organizing criticality as fundamental neural property.
2016 research on efficient hubs in salience network correlation with general intelligence.
Perennial philosophy perspective on shared spiritual themes across traditions.
Contemporary psychologist discovering the Solomon effect. Shows wiser reasoning on others' problems through third-person perspective. Demonstrates perspectival dimension of wisdom.
Researcher on the Solomon effect showing how de-centering enables wisdom and insight into interpersonal problems.
Philosopher whose work on sensibility transcendence (via John Wright) articulates mutual transformation in Agent-Arena relations.
Researcher with Ceci on cognitive leaping. Showed ability to complete patterns accurately with few cues is directly predictive of insight.
On I-me distinction; provides philosophical grounding for participatory symbol theory.
The Religious Case Against Belief; exploring religion beyond creeds.
Major defender of computational theory of mind who pointed out that inference presupposes relevance and that this undermines purely computational accounts.
A founding figure of Romanticism whose The Sorrows of Young Werther exemplifies Romantic irrationalism and its cultural influence.
Gospel writer who identifies agape with God itself, understanding Christian reality as participation in God's creative love.
Jungian analyst and theologian showing convergence between Jung and Tillich. The Psyche as Sacrament framework.
Co-authored work showing metaphor involves both bottom-up and top-down mechanisms.
On systematic import and inductive generalization; demonstrates why relevance cannot be made scientific.
Researcher on metacognitive insight in therapy. Argues mindfulness triggers insight by constraining S2 and foregrounding S1 processing.
On symbolic world; remembering functional and participatory nature of symbols.

Scandinavian psychologist distinguishing fallacy from misunderstanding. Shows these aren't cleanly separable, and determining which one occurred depends on understanding construal—a non-inferential process.
Historian who explored the Axial Revolution.
Philosopher who identified the Axial Age.
Kyoto School philosopher. Religion and Nothingness rated among top responses to meaning crisis. Integrates Heidegger, James, Buddhist thought.
Cognitive psychologist who provided the foundational example demonstrating combinatorial explosion in chess-playing.
Neuroscientists whose work on metastability explains how the brain achieves simultaneous integration and differentiation.
Co-author with Schwartz on practical wisdom. Emphasizes contextual sensitivity and judgment. Co-authored book Practical Wisdom.
Founder of Kyoto School. Engages Heidegger, James, and Buddhism to respond to meaning crisis through absolute nothingness.
Cognitive scientist who studies metaphor in human thought.
2012-2013 research showing small-world network organization correlates with working memory and intelligence.
Consciousness sets decision criteria for perception; perspectival knowing is primary guidance.
Consciousness functions to set decision criteria for perception based on situational awareness.
Demonstrated that categories are not stable across contexts; a set belongs together as a category in one context but not another, depending on what is relevant.
Collaborator on research connecting relevance realization to meaning and wisdom.
His critique that theology is the projection of human essence paves the way for Marx's materialist reinterpretation of Hegelian dialectics.
His music exemplifies Romanticism and its breaking of traditional forms, initially inspired by Napoleon as the embodiment of will.
Her research shows that working memory functions as a relevance filter to screen off irrelevant information.
Neuroscientist whose model of reciprocal narrowing in addiction illuminates the structure of dukkha and parasitic processing.
Roman emperor whose Meditations exemplify Stoic practice as spiritual exercises for transformation within circumstances of unlimited power.
Co-author on metaphorical structure of cognition; embodied metaphor theory.
New Science of Mind; extended mind and embodied phenomenology convergence.
His book After God provides a philosophical framework for understanding how the scientific revolution secularizes and fragments the integrated worldview Augustine had created.
Philosopher who distinguished I-It and I-Thou relationships.
On inter-categorical things as unclean/monstrous; purity codes based on categorical coherence.
Researcher on shamanism and its role in human cognitive development.
Phenomenologist integrating embodiment and embeddedness, closer to Heidegger's participatory knowing than Husserl.
On metaphor as more than comparison; involves interaction theory and salience imbalance.
His analysis of the Protestant Work Ethic shows how Luther's doctrine of arbitrary salvation drives obsessive labor as an unconscious sign of election.
Reflective equilibrium method; all wisdom theories converge on seeing through illusion into reality.
Scholar of shamanism across cultures and its neurological basis.
Augustine's Christian mother whose faith and the Bible she introduces become instruments of his transformation.

Psychological researcher critiquing Baltes and Staudinger's product theory of wisdom. Argues wisdom requires self-transcendence and participatory transformation. Identifies cognitive, reflective, and affective dimensions of wise persons.
2013 research showing consciousness linked to small-world network formation during anesthesia.
Argues that the frame problem has a technical solution but a deeper relevance problem remains: how do we know what matters?
His point about logical similarity (any two objects are indefinitely similar) reveals why categorization depends on psychological, not logical, similarity.
His nominalist principle—that universals are only mental constructs—provides the philosophical foundation that Galileo activates in physics, making arbitrary will primary to reason.
His creation of the German nation-state unifies fragmented territories, creating the conditions for fierce nationalism to fill the vacuum left by failed idealism.
On 'secular wonder' and the atmospheric bubble of significance that grounds meaning.

Co-author of foundational convergence argument showing philosophical and psychological theories of wisdom converge on systematic seeing through illusion. Work bridges Axial Age wisdom with contemporary cognitive science.
Originated self-organized criticality concept; foundational to understanding neural dynamics.
Neuroscientist on caring as fundamental difference between humans and computers.
Philosopher who articulated how trustworthiness, convergence, and elegance contribute to plausibility judgments.
Advocates cultivating poetic capacity for wonder and awe consonant with scientific worldview.
On wonder as emotion-to-spirituality transition; being mode vs. having mode curiosity.
Researcher on intuition and implicit learning in decision-making.
On the numinous as core of holiness; fascination and horror as polarities of sacredness.
Anthroposophist influencing Barfield's participatory imagination framework.
Romantic poet and philosopher influencing Barfield's imagination philosophy.
2005 mathematical model linking insight to conversion from regular to small-world networks.
Cognitive scientist studying prioritization in attention.
His critique that Hegel has made a system and then sat down beside it identifies the missing personal, transformative dimension.
First Christian martyr whose death and the forgiveness extended to Saul exemplify agapic transformation.
Work on disruptive strategies and entropy in insight; relates insight to SOC.
Co-researcher on pattern completion and insight prediction. Shows cognitive leaping is measurable and central to problem-solving.
2008-2009 research linking neural self-organization to intelligence measures.
Proposes artificial intelligence as the logical endpoint of the scientific worldview, arguing that matter rearranged sufficiently becomes mind.
Colleague who showed these strategies align with cutting-edge AI; co-authored paper on relevance realization.
Collaborator on work showing relevance realization as central to intelligence and cognition.
Contemporary interpreter of Corbin in Western context. Author of trilogy on Corbin's thought.
Developer of integrated information theory, which explains consciousness in terms of how powerfully integrated pieces of information are.
Sacred Depths of Nature; transcendence into nature; recovering sacredness in scientific worldview.
Communitas; unstructured community experiencing shared spirit and equality.
Buba-Kiki experiments showing synesthetic symbol perception; participatory nature of symbol engagement.
On essence and inductive generalization; distinguishes deductive from inductive essences.
On I-me distinction; the 'I' can never be captured as focal object, only known subsidiarily.
Physicist and Jung's collaborator on synchronicity, exploring connections between physics and psychology.
Machine learning researcher whose work shows disruption and noise are essential to self-optimization in neural networks.
Founder of Stoicism who integrates Cynic provocativeness with Platonic rationality, focusing on the process of co-identification.
Developed the theory of FINSTING (Fingers of Instantiation) showing how pre-categorical salience tagging precedes conceptual cognition.
